metadata.name: AntiVEGF-Guideline-KG
metadata.description: Multi-hop (Node-Edge-Node) clinical-guideline KG for anti-VEGF continuation. Extends v0.1 with an explicit 4-layer causal chain (Visual -> Pathophysiology -> Prognosis -> Decision) used to RENDER chain-of-thought SFT targets. Each node is supervised by its own ground truth: Visual=image biomarker labels, Prognosis=measured ΔCST category, Decision=recorded continue/stop label. The connective narrative is guideline-based (non-deterministic) so eyes whose response and decision diverge are rendered honestly rather than contradictorily.
metadata.method_source: Wang MH, Sensors 2025 25:6879 (neuro-symbolic causal/temporal reasoning + counterfactual). v0.2 adopts Wang's temporal-response framing: prognosis is a PREDICTED treatment-response node, not a static rule.
metadata.design_note: Input to the model is the PRE-treatment image ONLY. Prognosis (ΔCST response) is a PREDICTION TARGET the model must infer from the image, NOT an input hint. Its per-node accuracy is scored against measured ΔCST (informational-limit finding if unlearnable).

nodes.label: Active exudative disease
nodes.narrative: active exudative disease (intra/subretinal fluid leakage from an active neovascular or edematous process)

nodes.label: PED without active fluid
nodes.narrative: a pigment epithelial detachment without active intra- or sub-retinal fluid, an activity-indeterminate finding

nodes.label: Dry macula
nodes.narrative: a dry macula with no active exudation

nodes.label: Marked anatomic responder
nodes.narrative: a marked anatomic response to anti-VEGF (large reduction in central subfield thickness)

nodes.label: Partial responder
nodes.narrative: a partial anatomic response to anti-VEGF

nodes.label: No active disease to treat
nodes.narrative: no active disease for anti-VEGF to act on

guideline_rules.rationale: Subretinal fluid -> active exudation -> guideline favors continuation.
Intraretinal fluid -> active edema -> guideline favors continuation.
No active fluid (dry macula) -> guideline favors stopping.
PED only, no active fluid -> case-dependent.
